Name: efficient_inference_optimization
Title: Empirically Tuned Hardware-Aware Inference Optimization for Large Language Models
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that by combining empirically tuned heuristics, such as autotuned tile sizes and profiled per-layer precision, with hardware-aware optimizations, such as memory-bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, we can achieve significant improvements in inference latency and throughput on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: Our proposal differs from existing work, such as Spectral Latent Steering and Spectral Quantization Routing, in its focus on empirically tuned heuristics and hardware-aware optimizations, rather than relying on theoretical bounds or spectral geometry. Our approach is also distinct from Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding and CPU-GPU Workload Distribution, which focus on specific techniques for optimizing inference, whereas our proposal takes a more holistic approach to optimizing the entire inference pipeline.
Abstract: Large language models have achieved remarkable performance in various tasks, but their inference remains computationally expensive. We propose an empirically tuned hardware-aware inference optimization framework that combines autotuned tile sizes, profiled per-layer precision, and hardware-aware optimizations to improve inference latency and throughput on commodity GPUs. Our approach is based on a thorough analysis of the hardware characteristics and the model's computational requirements, allowing us to optimize the inference pipeline for maximum performance. We evaluate our framework on various large language models and demonstrate significant improvements in inference latency and throughput, making it suitable for real-world deployment scenarios.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on various large language models, such as LLaMA-7B and Code Llama, using different hardware configurations, including NVIDIA RTX A4000 and AMD Radeon Instinct MI8.
Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art inference optimization techniques, such as Spectral Latent Steering and Spectral Quantization Routing.
Investigate the impact of different tile sizes, precision settings, and hardware-aware optimizations on inference latency and throughput.
Analyze the energy consumption and memory usage of our framework and compare it with other optimization techniques.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require significant computational resources and expertise to implement and optimize.
The framework's performance may vary depending on the specific hardware configuration and model architecture.
The use of empirically tuned heuristics may lead to suboptimal performance in certain scenarios.
